+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8d80
+project: local-review
+title: Operation-based `review` MCP tool (thin wrapper over the engine)
+---
+## What
+Expose the engine as an OPERATION-BASED MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/`, dispatched by an `op` field exactly like the existing `git`, `kanban`, and `code_context` tools (single tool, op-dispatch — NOT a tool-per-verb). The tool is a thin wrapper: it parses the op + args, calls the engine `scope_review → run_fleet → verify_findings → synthesize`, and returns the structured `ReviewReport`. No orchestration logic lives in the tool.
+
+Ops (finalize names against the design doc, mirror the git tool's verb-noun convention):
+- `run review` — inputs: `{ range? , branch? , task_id? , dimensions? (subset), backend? ("session"|"local") }`; output: the `ReviewReport` (markdown + counts + the confirmed/refuted breakdown).
+- `list dimensions` — return the available dimensions and which rules feed each (so users can scope a review).
+- (optional, if cheap) `explain rule` — return a rule body by name.
+
+- Register via the existing `register_*_tools()` → `ToolRegistry` pattern used by the git tool; follow its op-dispatch module layout (`mod.rs` dispatch + per-op submodules) and its request/response serde structs.
+- Resolve the agent `ConnectionTo<Agent>` and the CWD from the MCP session/work-dir, NOT `std::env::current_dir()` (see the session-cwd-for-tools convention — a tool defaulting to CWD=/ walked the whole FS and hung the agent).
+- This tool does NOT write to kanban — the skill does that. The tool returns findings; the skill records them.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A single op-dispatched `review` tool is registered and discoverable over MCP, with `run review` and `list dimensions` ops.
+- [ ] `run review` runs scope→fleet→verify→synthesize and returns the `ReviewReport`; `backend` and `dimensions` inputs are honored.
+- [ ] Op-dispatch shape, registration, and request/response structs mirror the existing git tool; no tool-per-verb.
+- [ ] Agent connection + CWD resolved from the session/work-dir, never `current_dir()`.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Real-pipeline integration test (the reference pattern from `tests/integration/semantic_search_e2e.rs`): temp git repo with a planted duplicate + a planted dead function, drive `run review` through the registered tool with the mock/playback agent, assert the returned report flags both under the right dimensions and severities.
+- [ ] `list dimensions` returns the taxonomy.
+- [ ] Tool registration test (the op appears in the registry); `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools review` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the registered-tool integration test first (real registry, real engine, scripted agent), then wire the thin dispatch. Reuse the git tool's module structure as the template. This is a real-path test, not a mock-boundary unit test.
\ No newline at end of file
